description: Declarative mode registry: the single source of truth for the discriminator (workflowMode/packetKind/backendKind) PLUS the advisorRouting projection for cli-external-orchestration. The hub reads this file to resolve a mode; the advisor routes any external-CLI-dispatch query to the single identity cli-external-orchestration and the hub picks the mode. Both modes route by hub membership (routingClass 'metadata'), so there is no advisor map entry per mode and no merged-identity projection — the advisor does not read this file at runtime. This registry is also the executor-delegation scorer's source of truth for which CLI executors exist (system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/scorer/executor-delegation.ts), keyed by packetSkillName.

discriminator.workflowMode: Public hub/mode key. Stable identity used by the hub router and this registry. Carried by every mode.
discriminator.packetKind: The axis discriminator: 'workflow' for both cli-opencode and cli-claude-code — each classifies intent, chooses/confirms a provider, and conducts a dispatched CLI session whose writes land in this repo's workspace. Zero extensions (no surface-axis, no transport-axis): neither packet is read-only evidence, and both fail the transport contract because they orchestrate and their dispatched writes are local, not external.
discriminator.backendKind: Which backend runs the mode: cli-dispatch for both — each orchestrates the OpenCode or Claude Code CLI binary directly, with no MCP fallback path.

advisorRoutingContract.routingClass: How the advisor finds the mode. For cli-external-orchestration every mode is 'metadata' = resolved by hub membership; the advisor routes the single identity cli-external-orchestration and the hub picks the mode, so no mode carries an advisor map entry or a merged-identity projection.
advisorRoutingContract.packetSkillName: The packet's SKILL.md name. For cli-external-orchestration the packet folder IS its packetSkillName (folder cli-opencode == packetSkillName cli-opencode, and likewise for cli-claude-code) — so no mode has a folder/name mismatch and grandfatheredFolderMismatch is false throughout (ADR-002 full-rename decision).
advisorRoutingContract.grandfatheredFolderMismatch: true only when a packet folder differs from its packetSkillName (a preserved legacy mismatch). Both cli-external-orchestration modes use folder == packetSkillName, so grandfatheredFolderMismatch is false for both.
advisorRoutingContract.command: The bound slash command for the mode. Neither cli-opencode nor cli-claude-code has ever had a dedicated command (command:null on both, per ADR-003) — both stay advisor-routed and cross-skill-referenced only.
